highlights.text: This got us long and I wrote at 4:20PM last Monday: “I have added long exposure a little after the close ~6396 as we put in a nice Failed Breakdown of the 6396 low set at 2:40PM.” I also wrote: “My general lean though is always to defer to the trend.
I wrote on Friday at 4pm: “My general lean is always to defer to the trend.
The first part of this sequence is what I call “elevator down”.
This squeeze is - in turn - always caused by my core setup, the Failed Breakdown.
Specifically ES undercuts a low rapidly during the “elevator down” sell, traps shorts, then squeeze the other way.
It then went “elevator down” at 830AM to 6454.
We held 6454 exact at 830am Thursday, recovered 6461 (which was a Failed Breakdown key low from Tuesday), and then squeezed to 6507 by Friday morning.
Deja Vu: We started the day at 6507 and went “elevator down”, selling to 6461 instantly.
Elevator down, failed breakdown, short squeeze.
While it could trap below once more (no lower than a Failed Breakdown of Thursday’s low) as long as the structure defends after we simply work up the flag with 6478 then 6500 being targets.
In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll do a deep dive into some of the Failed Breakdowns we had last week as we had a large variety and its essential to be able to identify the different types.

post_title: For 3 Days Straight, SPX is Coiled Into The Tighest Range In Months. Breakout Is Close. What Way? August 19th Plan
